findings.title: Real-Time Co-Editing Feature Gap
findings.description: CollabDocs currently lacks real-time co-editing capabilities, which are now standard among competitors X and Y. This gap poses a critical risk as enterprise customers are actively seeking this feature, with three accounts already expressing urgency for simultaneous editing.
assumptions: Enterprise customers will churn if real-time co-editing is not implemented soon.

evidence.excerpt: Competitor X launched live co-editing on 2025-10-15 with a heavy marketing push directly targeting our enterprise segment.
Three enterprise accounts have submitted formal feature requests for simultaneous multi-user editing.

recommendations: Prioritize the development of real-time co-editing features to achieve parity with competitors.

findings.title: Competitive Positioning of Competitor X and Y
findings.description: Competitor X positions itself as a user-friendly tool with no additional costs for real-time editing, while Competitor Y targets enterprise clients with a focus on admin controls and audit trails. CollabDocs can leverage its strengths in document workflow to differentiate itself from these competitors.
assumptions: CollabDocs can attract users looking for workflow features alongside real-time editing.

evidence.excerpt: Competitor Y targets regulated industries that need audit trails and admin controls.
Competitor X's positioning emphasizes user-friendliness and no additional costs.

recommendations: Highlight CollabDocs' unique workflow features in marketing to attract users from both competitors.

findings.title: Risk of Customer Churn Due to Feature Gap
findings.description: With three enterprise accounts expressing urgent needs for simultaneous editing, there is a significant risk of churn if CollabDocs fails to deliver this feature promptly. The combined annual recurring revenue (ARR) at risk is $420K.
assumptions: Enterprise customers will switch to competitors if their needs are not met.

evidence.excerpt: Combined ARR at risk: $420K. Customer Success has escalated to Product with a 60-day window before formal churn conversations begin.

recommendations: Accelerate the development timeline for real-time co-editing to mitigate churn risk.

findings.title: Implement Conflict Resolution Strategy
findings.description: As CollabDocs moves towards real-time co-editing, it is essential to establish a formal conflict resolution strategy to prevent data loss during concurrent edits. Options include adopting either Operational Transformation (OT) or Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (CRDT).
assumptions: A robust conflict resolution strategy will enhance user experience and trust in the platform.

evidence.excerpt: A formal conflict resolution strategy is required as we move toward real-time co-editing.

recommendations: Evaluate both OT and CRDT approaches and select the one that best aligns with CollabDocs' document model.

summary: CollabDocs faces a critical gap in real-time co-editing capabilities, which is essential to retain enterprise customers and maintain competitive positioning. The analysis highlights the urgency of addressing this gap while leveraging existing strengths in document workflow features to differentiate from competitors.
